rationale.R1.1: The section makes a strong contribution claim ('strictly dominant deployment strategy'); assessing whether the framing and scope of that thesis are defensible given the reported evidence is a core review obligation.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the abstract asserts 'uniformly improved execution accuracy' but the benchmark table shows RouteSQL underperforms the baseline on WikiSQL (89.1% vs 89.6%) and SParC (64.2% vs 65.1%). This is a direct internal contradiction that invalidates a central premise of the argument.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the headline claim of uniform accuracy gains is directly contradicted by two of five reported benchmark figures. Evidence-claim alignment is fatally broken in the current draft and must be corrected or the framing must be revised.
rationale.R1.6: A generic 'review this section' instruction routinely includes sentence- and paragraph-level quality. The section also contains a rhetorical overreach ('strictly dominant') that compounds a writing-register concern on top of the logical one.
